subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, World Bank, UN/OCHA/MINUSCA, Freedom House, RSF, TI, HRW, Amnesty, Crisis Group, CSIS) + international press; state media EXCLUDED on governance.
Data limitation: among the world's least-developed states (HDI near the global floor) with sparse, lagged official data, much of it donor-run; confidence is reduced accordingly.

Standout drags: state security and gold/diamond extraction mortgaged to Russian Wagner/Africa Corps, an entrenched personalist regime (2023 term-limit removal, Touadera re-elected Dec 2025), active multi-front conflict, and near-total institutional absence outside Bangui. The CFA euro peg is the lone offset.

economic.notes: A peg-anchored but minimal-revenue, aid-dependent economy with extraction captured by Russian-linked operators.

fiscal_state.notes: High risk of debt distress; an IMF ECF on track with a narrowing deficit, but a minimal, aid-dependent revenue base. (IMF Jun 2025)

monetary_independence.notes: The CEMAC CFA euro peg with a French convertibility guarantee imports stability; no own policy. (BEAC; IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The CFA franc is regional, not a reserve currency; the 2022 Bitcoin/Sango legal-tender scheme collapsed. (Central Banking)

inflation.notes: Elevated but easing into 2025, with the CEMAC peg anchoring it relative to peers. (IMF Jun 2025)

banking_stability.notes: A tiny, shallow, cash-based sector within CEMAC with very low credit penetration. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Landlocked, single-corridor dependent (Douala via Cameroon), with gold/diamond/timber exports largely captured by Russian-linked operators. (CSIS; EITI)

institutional.notes: Pervasive impunity, near-absent state outside Bangui, sparse data, and a re-criminalized press.

rule_of_law.notes: Pervasive impunity, armed-group and Wagner-linked killings, and weak/absent state justice outside Bangui. (HRW WR2026; OHCHR)

statistical_integrity.notes: Sparse, lagged official data (the last poverty survey was 2021), with surveys largely donor-run. (World Bank)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Minimal reach beyond the capital - ~11% electricity access and only ~40% of health facilities operational. (WHO 2025; World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: RSF 'very serious' - a May 2025 re-criminalization of press offences and a pending 'foreign agents' bill. (RSF; HRW 2025)

political_social.notes: Open-ended personalist rule against a live insurgency and deep sectarian displacement.

political_stability.notes: Touadera consolidated via term-limit removal and Russian backing (re-elected Dec 2025), but CPC factions and Sudan spillover keep the insurgency live. (Crisis Group; UN SC)

civil_liberties.notes: Opposition rallies banned, MPs jailed despite immunity, and shrinking civic space. (Freedom House 2025; HRW)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Hosts some refugees but foreign artisanal miners have been targeted in Wagner-linked killings, with weak protection. (Al Jazeera 2024)

social_cohesion.notes: An anti-Balaka/ex-Seleka sectarian legacy, inter-communal violence, and ~455k+ IDPs plus ~680k refugees. (UNHCR/OCHA 2025)

demographics.notes: Very young and fast-growing (a long-run asset), but undermined by health/education collapse. (UN/World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: Regime survival mortgaged to Russia, with active multi-front conflict and Sudan spillover.

alliance_reliability.notes: Externally dependent on Russia (Africa Corps) for regime survival, with eroding Western/UN (MINUSCA) presence. (Crisis Group)

conflict_involvement.notes: Active multi-front internal conflict plus cross-border incursions from Sudan, with peacekeepers attacked. (UN SC; Global R2P 2025)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: A UN arms embargo eased, but Wagner-linked gold entities under US/EU sanctions, within CEMAC constraints. (US Treasury; UN)

physical_practical.notes: A barely-functioning health system, near-absent infrastructure, and armed-group insecurity.

climate.notes: Among the most vulnerable globally (ND-GAIN bottom-10) - conflict has collapsed adaptive capacity. (ND-GAIN)

healthcare.notes: Barely functioning - ~40% of facilities operational, NGO/MSF-dependent, with among the world's worst outcomes. (WHO/MSF 2024-25)

infrastructure.notes: ~11% electricity access, dirt roads impassable in rains, and no real public transport. (World Bank)

crime_safety.notes: Armed-group ambushes, banditry, and mining-zone violence. (HRW; Al Jazeera 2024)

disaster_insurance.notes: A negligible private insurance market. (inference, low data)

summary: One of the planet's poorest and most fragile states - HDI near the global floor, roughly two-thirds in extreme poverty, and a state whose security and key extractive sectors are mortgaged to Russia's Wagner/Africa Corps. The December 2025 election cemented Touadera's open-ended rule after the 2023 term-limit removal, against a boycotting, repressed opposition. Macro signals are modestly less bad (an IMF ECF on track, easing inflation under the CFA peg), but these sit atop active multi-front conflict, near-total institutional absence outside Bangui, and acute humanitarian need. The distribution is negatively skewed: the modal path is grinding fragility, with a fat tail toward renewed civil war or a rupture in the Russia relationship.
